issues.description: QuickLinks.php manually extracts attributes from $attributes array (lines 10-13). The theme's inc/helpers/blocks.php automatically calls extract($attributes, EXTR_SKIP) before including PHP templates, making manual extraction redundant and against project conventions.
issues.fix: Remove lines 10-13 (manual extraction). Add @var docblock at top of file listing all camelCase attribute variables to prevent PHP_CodeSniffer warnings. Use variables directly with null coalescing for safe defaults: $eyebrow ?? '', $heading ?? '', etc.

issues.description: QuickLinkItem.php manually extracts attributes from $attributes array (lines 10-13). Same issue as FQA-001 - attributes are auto-extracted by the theme helper, manual extraction is redundant.
issues.fix: Remove lines 10-13 (manual extraction). Add @var docblock at top of file for camelCase variables. Use variables directly: if ( empty( $title ?? '' ) || empty( $url ?? '' ) )

issues.description: QuickLinks.php and QuickLinkItem.php are missing @var docblocks for camelCase attribute variables. Without these docblocks, PHP_CodeSniffer will warn about camelCase variables ($eyebrow, $opensInNewTab, etc.) because WordPress coding standards prefer snake_case. The docblock tells the linter these variables come from block attributes (which use camelCase from JavaScript) and should be ignored.
issues.lines: Top of file after opening comment
issues.fix:
Add comprehensive @var docblock listing all camelCase attribute variables. Example:

<?php
/**
 * Quick Links block frontend template.
 *
 * Available variables (auto-extracted from $attributes):
 * @var string $anchor
 * @var string $eyebrow
 * @var string $heading
 * @var string $description
 * @var array $buttons
 *
 * Also available:
 * @var string $children Inner blocks content
 * @var WP_Block $block Block instance
 */
?>
issues.reference: See .taktician/agents/functional-qa-agent.md Phase 8.1 - Why the docblock is important

codeReviewFindings.accessibilityImplementation: All 5 accessibility improvements from the planning spec successfully implemented in code. Nav landmark, list semantics, aria-label, focus indicator preservation, and sr-only external link text all verified in source files.
codeReviewFindings.structuralSync: TSX and PHP structures are correctly synchronized. Both use <section> wrapper, container grid, <nav> with aria-label, <ul> wrapper, and <li> items. Element order and conditional rendering patterns match.
codeReviewFindings.attributeExtraction: Critical anti-pattern found: Both PHP templates manually extract attributes despite theme auto-extraction. This is a major sync violation requiring developer fix.
codeReviewFindings.focusManagement: Correctly implemented - no focus:outline-none in either TSX or PHP. Focus states use accent colors alongside browser default focus ring.
codeReviewFindings.srOnlyText: PHP correctly implements sr-only span for external links. TSX uses Link component which may handle this internally (cannot verify without component source).

recommendations: Fix FQA-001 and FQA-002 (major severity) by removing manual attribute extraction from both PHP templates
Add @var docblocks to both PHP templates (minor severity) to prevent PHP_CodeSniffer warnings
Once WordPress API is restored, create test page with all 7 scenarios from planning spec
Run taktician_page_audit on rendered test page to verify DOM structure matches expectations
Run taktician_screenshot with hover/focus actions to verify visual states
Test with screen reader to verify nav landmark announcements and sr-only text
